paper_title: Measurement of the fracture toughness of polycrystalline bubbly ice from an Antarctic ice core

methods.description: This method measures the Mode I fracture toughness (K Ic) of Antarctic ice using a four-point bending technique at -15°C. The fracture toughness is a material parameter that characterizes a material's resistance to crack growth, which is crucial for predicting material failure.

steps.description: Prepare the ice sample and notch it according to the ASTM (2008) E 1820-08 standard.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and notch ice sample according to ASTM E 1820-08 standard

steps.description: Load the notched ice sample to failure under displacement control with a rate of approximately 65 µm/s.
steps.searchable_summary: load ice sample to failure under displacement control

steps.description: Measure displacement, load, and time at a frequency of 50 Hz using a LabVIEW program.
steps.searchable_summary: measure displacement, load, and time using LabVIEW

steps.description: Determine the critical fracture toughness using the maximum measured force, sample geometry, and positioning of the loading rollers.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate critical fracture toughness from maximum force and sample geometry
